title: The Tax Bracket Myth That Makes You Refuse Money
hook: Millions of workers turn down raises because they think a higher bracket will leave them with less — and that belief is completely, mathematically wrong.
angle: Exposes one of the most persistent personal finance myths using concrete math: you can never take home less by earning more. Only the marginal dollars above the threshold get taxed at the higher rate.
audience: Anyone who has ever hesitated to take a raise, bonus, or extra income due to fear of moving into a higher tax bracket
key_numbers: $8,000 average annual cost of the myth
$3,900 you still keep from a $5,000 raise at worst-case 22% rate
$1,100 in extra taxes — not a salary wipeout
